2604.19292.title: Location Not Found: Exposing Implicit Local and Global Biases in Multilingual LLMs
2604.19292.paper_summary: Multilingual large language models (LLMs) have minimized the fluency gap between languages. This advancement, however, exposes models to the risk of biased behavior, as knowledge and norms may propagate across languages. In this work, we aim to quantify models' inter- and intra-lingual biases, via their ability to answer locale-ambiguous questions. To this end, we present LocQA, a test set containing 2,156 questions in 12 languages, referring to various locale-dependent facts such as laws, dates, and measurements. The questions do not contain indications of the locales they relate to, other than the querying language itself. LLMs' responses to LocQA locale-ambiguous questions thus reveal models' implicit priors. We used LocQA to evaluate 32 models, and detected two types of structural biases. Inter-lingually, we show a global bias towards answers relevant to the US-locale, even when models are asked in languages other than English. Moreover, we discovered that this global bias is exacerbated in models that underwent instruction tuning, compared to their base counterparts. Intra-lingually, we show that when multiple locales are relevant for the same language, models act as demographic probability engines, prioritizing locales with larger populations. Taken together, insights from LocQA may help in shaping LLMs' desired local behavior, and in quantifying the impact of various training phases on different kinds of biases.
2604.19292.paper_summary_zh: 多語言的大型語言模型（LLMs）已經最小化了語言之間的流利度差距。 然而，這一進展使模型面臨偏見行為的風險，因為知識和規範可能會在語言之間傳播。 在這項工作中，我們旨在量化模型的語言間和語言內偏見，通過它們回答地區模糊問題的能力。 為此，我們提出了LocQA，一個包含12種語言的2,156個問題的測試集，涉及各種依賴地區的事實，如法律、日期和測量。 除了查詢語言本身之外，這些問題不包含與其相關的地區指示。 因此，LLMs對LocQA地區模糊問題的回答揭示了模型的隱性先驗。 我們使用LocQA評估了32個模型，並檢測到兩種類型的結構性偏見。 在語言間，我們顯示出對與美國地區相關的答案存在全球偏見，即使在使用非英語的語言詢問模型時也是如此。 此外，我們發現，相較於其基礎版本，經過指令調整的模型這一全球偏見更加嚴重。 在語言內，我們顯示當多個地區對同一語言相關時，模型充當人口概率引擎，優先考慮人口較多的地區。 總之，來自LocQA的見解可能有助於塑造LLMs所需的本地行為，並量化不同訓練階段對各種偏見的影響。
2604.19292.author: Guy Mor-Lan et.al.
2604.19292.authors: Guy Mor-Lan, Omer Goldman, Matan Eyal, Adi Mayrav Gilady, Sivan Eiger, Idan Szpektor, Avinatan Hassidim, Yossi Matias, Reut Tsarfaty
